steps.title: Spot the convention shift
steps.content: The passage first says the poems often follow sonnet conventions, including fourteen-line length. The specific twelve-line poem then subverts those conventions, so the transition must signal contrast.

steps.title: Reject continuation signals
steps.content:
• Choice A would make the twelve-line poem an example of following the convention.
  • Choice B signals similarity, but the poem breaks the pattern.
  • Choice D signals aptness rather than opposition.

steps.title: Mark the contrast
steps.content: By contrast correctly marks the shift from poems that adhere to convention to a poem that playfully subverts it. The answer is C.
